phase_coverage.title: Lock Current Version

requirements.item: Use current main as the Phase 6 validation baseline.
requirements.conclusion: Current branch is main; latest local health replay is recorded for f90c032070bd0d2b2bfc8fd84928b37c46173980.

requirements.item: Keep feature growth frozen: no memory schema, cognitive layer, or CLI expansion.
requirements.conclusion: The latest health replay records memory_schema_changed=false, cognitive_layer_changed=false, and cli_feature_changed=false.

requirements.item: Default heavy validation to CUDA device 0.
requirements.conclusion: Pinned official-style DMR runs record accelerator=cuda and cuda_device_id=0. This is supported for the current heavy DMR evidence, while future heavy reruns still need the same gate.
remaining: Continue requiring --accelerator cuda --cuda-device-id 0 for every future heavy rerun.

requirements.item: Record every experiment in SYSTEM_VALIDATION_REPORT and EXPERIMENT_LOG.
requirements.conclusion: Current audits and replay evidence are logged in the Phase 6 docs.

phase_coverage.conclusion: The current validation baseline is locked enough for continued Phase 6 work.
phase_coverage.next_action: Keep feature freeze and continue evidence-only validation.

phase_coverage.title: Official-Style DMR

requirements.item: Add answer generation from recalled memory chunks.
requirements.conclusion: Pinned DMR reports include generator.policy=extractive and answer_generation aggregates.

requirements.item: Score generated answers with exact, punctuation-normalized, ROUGE-L, and LLM judge.
requirements.conclusion: DMR 50 and 200 contain all required scoring surfaces; the 500-request run contains the same scoring on its 323 mappable samples.

requirements.item: Run DMR 50, then DMR 200 / 500.
requirements.conclusion: DMR 50 and 200 are fully selected/scored; DMR 500 is honestly a 500-request / 323-scored local view because punctuation mapping rejects 177 rows before scoring.
remaining: Do not claim 500/500 coverage under the current punctuation mapping policy.

requirements.item: Decide whether weakness is retrieval/ranking or answer synthesis.
requirements.conclusion: Official-style DMR is blocked by three separate bottlenecks: mapping coverage, retrieval/ranking, and answer synthesis. The largest pinned local view has 177 punctuation-mapping rejections before scoring, 114 scored samples without a relevant top-10 retrieval, and 118 top-1 retrieval hits whose extractive answer still lacks the gold substring.

requirements.item: Classify DMR 500 failure modes into major buckets.
requirements.conclusion: DMR 500 top-context repeats the positive answer-synthesis direction, but most requested rows remain unresolved under the current local official-style policy.

requirements.item: Explain whether mapping rejects imply empty memory chunks or scoring-policy boundary.
requirements.conclusion: The 177 punctuation-rejected rows are mostly a mapping/scoring boundary, not evidence of empty memory chunks.

requirements.item: Check whether top-context DMR gains are stable and paired-significant across scale views.
requirements.conclusion: Each scale has more candidate-only correct rows than baseline-only correct rows, and the exact McNemar test is significant at 0.05 on all three scale views.

requirements.item: Judge-score top-context candidate before making stronger answer-quality claims.
requirements.conclusion: DMR 50, DMR 200, and the 500-request / 323-scored top-context candidates are now judge-scored.
remaining: Improve answer quality and finalize published-comparable DMR policy before broader answer-quality claims.

phase_coverage.conclusion: Official-style DMR is executable and judge-backed for the pinned extractive baseline plus the DMR 50/200/500-request top-context candidates, but it is not published-comparable and absolute answer quality is still low.
phase_coverage.next_action: Stop expanding DMR judge scaling; move to hosted external comparison or no-model failure analysis.

phase_coverage.title: Ranking Without Architecture Change

requirements.item: Fix LongMemEval 50 and DMR 50 and classify failures.
requirements.conclusion: DMR 50 and LongMemEval 50 remain the fixed small-sample ranking views; DMR failure buckets are recorded.

requirements.item: Run one-variable ranking tests: RRF, vector weight, reranker pool, top-k, chunk, query expansion.
requirements.conclusion: Current reports cover the named one-variable ranking families and consolidate their conflicts/tradeoffs.

requirements.item: Do not set a default if a parameter helps one dataset and hurts another.
requirements.conclusion: Existing one-variable ranking evidence does not support a new global default. RRF k is mostly flat, vector weight improves coverage with MRR/top-1 tradeoffs, reranker-pool preferences diverge between DMR and LongMemEval, and pool-signal guards have no screened safe default.

requirements.item: Check whether LongMemEval and DMR trends stay consistent as samples expand.
requirements.conclusion: DMR top-context answer generation is stable and paired-significant, but LongMemEval and DMR ranking trends are not aligned enough to support a global runtime ranking default.
remaining: The Phase 6 trend-alignment exit condition is not complete for a global runtime default. The correct evidence-backed decision is validation-only: keep feature freeze, do not change runtime defaults, and require either an explicit DMR/LongMemEval objective split or a new answer-free ordering signal.

The LongMemEval / DMR ranking conflict is best treated as an objective split, not as a core architecture failure. DMR top-context answer generation is stable, but cross-dataset ranking evidence does not support a global runtime default.

requirements.item: Decide whether DMR / LongMemEval ranking conflict is an architecture failure or objective split.
requirements.conclusion: The LongMemEval / DMR ranking conflict is best treated as an objective split, not as a core architecture failure. DMR top-context answer generation is stable, but cross-dataset ranking evidence does not support a global runtime default.

requirements.item: Find a safe global ranking default.
requirements.conclusion: Ranking is a validated bottleneck, but current one-variable evidence does not support a global runtime default.
remaining: Need a new answer-free ordering signal or separately validated objective-specific policies.

phase_coverage.conclusion: Ranking is a validated bottleneck, but no runtime default change is supported.
phase_coverage.next_action: Keep the split as validation-only evidence. Continue hosted external comparison when credentials/endpoints are configured; otherwise continue no-model failure analysis or design a new answer-free ordering signal without changing runtime defaults.

phase_coverage.title: External Fair Comparison

requirements.item: Use the same cognitive fixture and mark unsupported separately from errors.
requirements.conclusion: The local comparison report records hit, unsupported, not_configured, and failed counts separately.

requirements.item: Check Synapse trace capabilities against measured adapters.
requirements.conclusion: King Synapse records 8/8 local hits for dominant trace, suppressed alternatives, evidence paths, future continuation, and reinforcement isolation in the shared fixture.
remaining: Local adapter evidence is not the same as hosted Graphiti/Zep, official Mem0, or live Letta.

requirements.item: Run Letta endpoint, hosted/standard Graphiti/Zep, and official Mem0.
requirements.conclusion: Hosted report has measured_systems=1 and not_configured_systems=3.
remaining: Configure Letta API/base URL or LETTA_ENVIRONMENT=local.

phase_coverage.conclusion: The local cognitive-trace comparison is useful but hosted/official fairness is still blocked.
phase_coverage.next_action: Provide hosted competitor credentials/endpoints, then rerun the shared fixture.

phase_coverage.title: Long-Horizon Stability

requirements.item: Construct multi-day memory data and test old/new memory influence.
requirements.conclusion: The deterministic long-horizon fixture records fixed metrics at 1.000 for RecallAt10, HebbianConsistency, and CognitiveTraceDominance.

requirements.item: Trace hidden influence and reinforcement drift over time.
requirements.conclusion: Candidate presence remains 8/8 and the evidence audit localizes the two misses to target-side context overlap.

requirements.item: Prove real-world/public long-memory stability.
requirements.conclusion: Long-horizon deterministic evidence supports stable network-memory behavior, but it does not yet support public real-world long-memory or product claims.
remaining: Add broader long-memory evidence only as validation work.

phase_coverage.conclusion: The time-network thesis is supported in the deterministic fixture, with future matched-evidence labeling still the weak surface.
phase_coverage.next_action: Keep the regression gate fixed and broaden only after current gates are clean.

phase_coverage.title: Productization Decision

requirements.item: Know what Synapse is clearly strong at.
requirements.conclusion: Strongest supported claim is cognitive-trace introspection in local fixtures.

requirements.item: Know where it is weaker than Mem0 / Graphiti / Zep.
requirements.conclusion: Local external comparison supports Synapse's inspectable cognitive-trace advantage on the shared fixture, but hosted/official competitor comparison is still not configured.

requirements.item: Know which capabilities others do not expose.
requirements.conclusion: Local comparison supports dominant trace, suppressed alternatives, future continuation, and reinforcement isolation as differentiators.

requirements.item: Decide whether GPU cost is acceptable.
requirements.conclusion: GPU memory and reranker latency are measured, but no product acceptance threshold has been adopted.

requirements.item: Ensure official DMR supports README claims.
requirements.conclusion: Pinned official-style DMR exists, but answer quality is low, mapping coverage is partial, and top-context judge scaling is complete.

requirements.item: Have a stable demo before web/API/Docker/release work.
requirements.conclusion: Productization is a no-go. Synapse has validated local cognitive-trace and deterministic long-horizon strengths, but official DMR, hosted external comparison, safe runtime defaults, public long-memory evidence, GPU cost acceptance, and public demo readiness are not closed.

requirements.item: Select the next heavy validation action only when external preconditions are ready.
requirements.conclusion: DMR 50/200/500 top-context judge scoring is complete; no further DMR heavy branch is currently selected by this gate.
remaining: Configure hosted competitor comparison or continue no-model failure analysis.

phase_coverage.next_action: Do not start web demo, API server, Docker, release packaging, or v0.1; follow the next-action gate and wait for external preconditions.

read.strongest_current_result: Synapse has strong local evidence for inspectable cognitive traces and executable official-style DMR scoring, but the decisive published-comparable and hosted-comparison gates are still open.
read.next_action: Keep feature freeze. DMR 50/200/500 top-context judge scoring is complete; the next heavy validation action waits for hosted competitor credentials/endpoints.

limits: This report is a coverage audit over existing evidence only.
It does not run retrieval, generation, judging, hosted adapters, or product code.
It should not be used as a product-readiness claim.
